subject: Q3 report — need your section by Thursday
body: Hi Umair, can you send me your part of the Q3 report by end of day Thursday? I need it merged before the Friday leadership review. Just your metrics section, nothing else. Thanks!

subject: 🔥 10 VS Code extensions you're sleeping on
body: This week's roundup is here! Our editors picked the 10 extensions that quietly changed how they code. Plus: a deep dive on terminal multiplexers and a reader poll on your favorite theme. Read online or unsubscribe anytime at the link below.

subject: FYI: staging deploy complete
body: Heads up team — staging is now on build 481 with the latest changes from main. No action needed from anyone, just letting you know in case you're testing there this afternoon.

subject: Invite: Capstone check-in — Tue 3:00 PM
body: Hi Umair, I've set up a 30-minute check-in for your capstone progress on Tuesday at 3:00 PM in Meeting Room 2. Please accept if that works, or suggest another slot if you're tied up. Agenda: agent design walkthrough.

subject: football saturday?
body: yo are you free saturday morning? the usual pitch is booked 10am, few of the guys are coming. let me know if you're in so i can confirm numbers

subject: thoughts on the onboarding doc?
body: Hey, when you get a chance, would love your thoughts on the onboarding doc I shared last week. No rush at all — just curious whether the setup section makes sense to a fresh pair of eyes. Cheers.

subject: Your July newsletter + a note about your plan
body: Welcome to the July edition! Inside: new data-center regions, a guide to caching, and customer spotlights. One quick note tucked in here — the card on file for your KVM 2 VPS expires this month, so update your payment details before the 28th to avoid any interruption to your service. Happy hosting!

subject: 🔴 Monitor DOWN: devmair.com
body: Your monitor devmair.com (HTTPS) is currently DOWN. It stopped responding at 2026-07-14 05:12 UTC with error: Connection Timeout. We'll notify you when it's back up. This is an automated alert.

subject: Re: Re: Re: API rate limits discussion
body: Thanks all for the back-and-forth on this. Agreed the current ceiling is too low. To wrap up: I think we go with the token-bucket approach Ahmed sketched. Umair, can you draft the config change and open a PR by Wednesday so we can review before the sprint closes? Everything else in the thread we can park for now.
